title: 🎙️ Repair Culture & Practical Tech
description: Today is about things you can fix, keep running, and pass on — repair culture, right-to-repair wins and setbacks, gear that outlives its manufacturer's support window, and the maker ethos of building to last rather than building to replace. We're interested in tools, devices, and projects where longevity, repairability, and self-sufficiency intersect, especially in rural and off-grid contexts where you can't just run to a repair shop. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today is about things you can fix, keep running, and pass on — repair culture, right-to-repair wins and setbacks, gear that outlives its manufacturer's support window, and the maker ethos of building to last rather than building to replace. We're interested in tools, devices, and projects where longevity, repairability, and self-sufficiency intersect, especially in rural and off-grid contexts where you can't just run to a repair shop.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt:
Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Repair Culture & Practical Tech' — the right to repair movement, gear that outlasts its manufacturer, DIY fixes, and tools built for longevity.

CALIBRATION — use this ladder:
- 80-100: Directly about repairability, right-to-repair legislation wins or losses, teardowns scoring repairability (iFixit-style), gear designed for longevity or user serviceability, products outliving manufacturer support windows and how owners keep them running, permacomputing, vintage or legacy hardware still in active use, open-source hardware enabling long-term repairability, Louis Rossmann-style repair advocacy, 3D-printed replacement parts extending device life, battery replacement guides, rural or off-grid repair self-sufficiency.
- 55-79: Hands-on maker and DIY content (builds, modifications, hardware hacks, workshop projects), practical consumer electronics guides with real technical depth, 3D printing hardware and filament reviews, off-grid and solar power gear reviews, home lab hardware, tools and workshop equipment, battery and power tech — even without an explicit right-to-repair angle.
- 30-54: General gadget and device reviews or news with some hands-on or technical substance; consumer electronics that touch on software longevity or ecosystem lock-in; hardware products with open firmware or modular design as a selling point.
- 10-29: Surface-level product announcements, consumer gadget speculation without technical depth, leaked device specs, general tech news without a hardware or repair angle.
- 0-9: Geopolitical conflict, natural disaster (without engineering/preparedness angle), wildlife and conservation, working lands policy, Indigenous governance, science discoveries without DIY or hardware relevance, financial market news.

Strongly prefer: articles about the right-to-repair movement (legislation, court cases, company policies), repairability scores and teardowns, gear with documented longevity, open-source or user-serviceable hardware, stories of devices kept alive past manufacturer support end-of-life, and rural/off-grid self-sufficiency through repair. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [New Atlas] FAA updates regulations for new supersonic age
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

The US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) is clearing the way for the return of commercial supersonic air travel by revamping regulations for faster-than-sound aircraft for the first time since 1973 – to reflect progress in aviation engineering and the removal of prejudicial attitudes.

Continue Reading

Category: Aircraft, Transport

Tags: FAA, Supersonic, NASA, X-Plane, Aircraft

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Massive study links teen marijuana use to double the risk of serious mental illness
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

Teens who use cannabis may face a substantially greater risk of developing serious mental health conditions, including psychotic and bipolar disorders, according to a study of more than 463,000 adolescents. Researchers found cannabis use often preceded these diagnoses by nearly two years, strengthening concerns about its long-term effects on developing brains.

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Astronomers found two rare super puff planets lighter than cotton candy
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

Two newly confirmed "super-puff" planets are so diffuse that they are less dense than cotton candy, despite being about the size of Jupiter. Their rare orbital relationship and enormous, lightweight atmospheres could provide valuable clues about how some of the strangest planets in the galaxy come to exist.
items.summary: Two newly confirmed "super-puff" planets are so diffuse that they are less dense than cotton candy, despite being about the size of Jupiter. items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] ‘It’s open green space versus construction’: Delhi’s historic polo club and the battle for the city’s future
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

The government’s requisition of a historic green space has ignited a fierce debate about air quality and heat stress in India’s scorching capital

For decades, the social highlight of winters in Delhi for the “beautiful people” was the polo season. A sprinkling of royalty and diplomats, impeccably groomed women in pearls and chiffon saris, along with wealthy industrialists sporting silk pocket squares used to gather to watch polo players compete under the mild, balmy sun.

They cheered on handsome players who, once the match was over, had children shrieking in delight as they put on a heart-stopping display of tent-pegging derring-do. Swish champagne lunches and other après-polo celebrations followed.

Continue reading...

items.title: [CFJC Today Kamloops] NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

File photo. (Image Credit: CFJC Today) By Aaron Schulze NorthPaws NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons Jul 3, 2026 | 6:50 AM Share on Facebook Share on Bluesky Share on X Copy Link KAMLOOPS — The Kamloops NorthPaws dropped their three-game home-and-home-and-home series with the Kelowna Falcons. The NorthPaws were blown out by the Falcons on the road 19-7 Thursday (July 2) to close out the three-game set. It comes after dropping the first game in Kelowna on Tuesday 11-6. Overall, the NorthPaws are 0-4 in Kelowna in the 2026 West Coast League (WCL) season.

items.title: [New Atlas] Google looks to electronic waste as cost-effective AI server solution
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

Every year, billions of phones are discarded globally, many of them with perfectly usable processors. At the same time, the tech industry is preparing to spend billions on new AI computing hardware, at high environmental costs. Google, in collaboration with researchers at the University of California, San Diego, is developing a way to bridge those two realities by building a server from recovered phone processors, tackling a waste problem while providing low-carbon computing.

Continue Reading

Category: Computing, Consumer Tech, Technology

Tags: Processing, Mobile Phones, Cell+Phone, Chips
